popularStory.headline: William de Braose's eldest son, William (IV)
popularStory.description:
«b»Biography«/b»
«b»Marriage«/b»
William de Braose's eldest son, William (IV) , married Maud (Matilda) de Clare (ca. 1184-1213), the daughter of Richard de Clare, 3rd Earl of Hertford.

zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Braose IV於大約1175年出生在英國素可布拉波時，他的父親William de Braose23歲，母親Maud de St Valéry Lady of Bramber21歲。他於1197年與Matilda de Clare在英國英格蘭諾福爾可海頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1210年4月9日在英國多瑟特可費 卡素享年36歲，埋葬在英國多瑟特可費 卡素。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Braose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Eckhard, Gerhard, Manfred, Otto.
from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Ambrosius (see Ambrose ).

variants.name: Ambrose
meanings: English and Scottish: from the personal name Ambrose (French Ambroise, Latin Ambrosius, from Greek ambrosios ‘immortal, divine’), which was popular throughout Christendom in medieval Europe. Its popularity was due in part to the fame of Saint Ambrose (c. 340–397), one of the four Latin Fathers of the Church, the teacher of Saint Augustine. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Ambrož (see Ambroz ), and also their derivatives, e.g. the Slovenian patronymic Ambrožič.
Irish: from Mac Ambróis ‘son of Ambrose’ (see 1 above); a West Munster name, which has also been Anglicized as McCambridge .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Braose IV was born about 1175, in Bramber, Sussex, England, his father, William de Braose, was 23 and his mother, Maud de St Valéry Lady of Bramber, was 21. He married Matilda de Clare in 1197, in Heydon, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 9 April 1210, in Corfe Castle, Dorset, England, at the age of 36, and was buried in Corfe Castle, Dorset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Braose IV est né environ 1175, à Bramber, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, William de Braose, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Maud de St Valéry Lady of Bramber, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Matilda de Clare en 1197, à Heydon, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 9 avril 1210, à Corfe Castle, Dorset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 36 ans, et a été enterré à Corfe Castle, Dorset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de Braose IV um 1175 in Bramber, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Braose 23 und seine Mutter Maud de St Valéry Lady of Bramber 21. Er hat 1197 in Heydon, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Matilda de Clare geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 9. April 1210 im Alter von 36 Jahren in Corfe Castle, Dorset, England und wurde in Corfe Castle, Dorset, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Braose IVが約1175年にイギリススセックスブランバーで生まれた時，父William de Braoseは23歳，母Maud de St Valéry Lady of Bramberは21歳であった。1197年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクヘイドンでMatilda de Clareと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1210年4月9日にイギリスドールセトコルフ カストルで36歳で死去し，イギリスドールセトコルフ カストルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Braose IV(이)가 대략 1175년 영국 수세스 브램버에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Braose(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud de St Valéry Lady of Bramber(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1197년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 헤이던에서 Matilda de Clare(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1210년 4월 9일 영국 도르세트 코르프 카스틀에서 36세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 도르세트 코르프 카스틀에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Braose IV родился около 1175 г. в местности брэмбир, суссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени William de Braose было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Maud de St Valéry Lady of Bramber, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Matilda de Clare в 1197 г. в местности хидон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 9 апреля 1210 г. в местности корф кастл, дорсет, Англия в возрасте 36 лет и был похоронен в местности корф кастл, дорсет, Англия.
